merger_name: CVC Capital Partners and Groupe Bruxelles Lambert – Recordati

targets.name: Recordati Industria Chimica e Farmaceutica S.p.A. / Recordati S.p.A.

anzsic_codes.name: Human Pharmaceutical and Medicinal Product Manufacturing

merger_description:
The transaction concerns the proposed acquisition of joint control of Recordati S.p.A. (**Recordati**) by Groupe Bruxelles Lambert SA (together with its subsidiaries, **GBL**) alongside the CVC Funds (defined below) through Respighi Bidco S.p.A. (**BidCo**) (the **Acquisition**). Recordati is currently solely controlled by CVC Funds.

The CVC Network is a global alternative investment manager focused on private equity, credit, infrastructure and secondaries, and includes CVC Capital Partners plc (**CVC plc**), each of its subsidiaries from time to time and the CVC Funds. CVC plc is listed on the Amsterdam Stock Exchange. As it relates to the CVC Network’s private equity activities, CVC plc indirectly, through certain of its subsidiaries from time to time, provide investment advice to and/or manages investments on behalf of certain funds and investment vehicles (the **CVC Funds**).

GBL is a publicly listed investment holding company headquartered in Brussels. GBL invests in global companies operating in various sectors, mainly headquartered in Europe, and relies on a stable and supportive family shareholder base.

Headquartered in Milan, Recordati is an international pharmaceutical group dedicated to the research, development, manufacturing and marketing of pharmaceuticals. Recordati has operations throughout the whole of Europe, Turkey, North Africa, the US, Canada, Mexico, South America, Japan, Australia, New Zealand, China and South Korea.

Recordati offers a range of pharmaceuticals across two segments: (i) Specialty & Primary Care and (ii) Rare Diseases. Recordati also produces several active ingredients and intermediates in its two pharmaceutical chemical production plants. These are mainly used in the production of some of the key products in the portfolio, but in part are also sold externally to other pharmaceutical companies.

CVC has established BidCo to launch a voluntary tender offer over all outstanding Recordati shares. BidCo is wholly and indirectly owned by Respighi Topco Sarl (**TopCo**). Following the Acquisition, GBL will have a shareholding of approximately 30% (subject to change but will not amount to 50% or more) in TopCo with the acquiring CVC Funds holding the remainder, subject to a small amount of shares held by Mr Andrea Recordati.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Groupe Bruxelles Lambert SA (together with its subsidiaries, GBL),
alongside certain funds managed and/or advised by CVC Capital
Partners plc (the CVC Funds) indirectly, applied, through Respighi
Bidco S.p.A (Bidco), for a notification waiver in respect of their
proposed acquisition of all outstanding shares of Recordati S.p.A.
(Recordati), as described in the transaction documents provided as
part of the application (the Acquisition).
Following completion of the Acquisition, GBL will have a
shareholding of approximately 30% in TopCo with the acquiring CVC
Funds holding the remainder, subject to a small amount of shares
held by Mr Andrea Recordati.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, Bidco, is indirectly but wholly owned by Respighi Topco
Sarl (TopCo). TopCo is in turn held by the CVC Funds. The CVC
Network is a global alternative investment manager focused on
private equity, credit, infrastructure and secondaries, and includes
CVC Capital Partners plc, each of its subsidiaries from time to time
and the CVC Funds. Relevantly, the CVC network’s portfolio includes
interests in companies active within the pharmaceutical industry.
GBL is a publicly listed investment holding company headquartered
in Brussels. GBL invests in global companies operating in various
sectors, mainly headquartered in Europe, and relies on a stable and
supportive family shareholder base.
The target, Recordati, is headquartered in Milan and is an
international pharmaceutical group dedicated to the research,
development, manufacturing and marketing of pharmaceuticals.
Recordati has operations throughout the whole of Europe, Turkey,
North Africa, the US, Canada, Mexico, South America, Japan,
Australia, New Zealand, China and South Korea.
Recordati offers a range of pharmaceuticals across two segments:
(i) Specialty & Primary Care and (ii) Rare Diseases. Recordati also
produces several active ingredients and intermediates in its two
pharmaceutical chemical production plants. These are mainly used
in the production of some of the key products in the portfolio, but in
part are also sold externally to other pharmaceutical companies.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application, and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers
that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening
of competition. In particular,
a. the CVC Funds already control Recordati, and the Acquisition
does not create any new horizontal overlap between CVC
Capital Partners and Recordati in the supply of
pharmaceuticals in Australia
b. there is no horizontal overlap between GBL’s portfolio
companies and Recordati in the supply of pharmaceuticals
in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
